The author's position seems to be in favor of the traditional local pub, not necessarily the gastropub. So option A is probably not correct. Option B: Wine is thought to show one's class and taste. The article mentions that wine is often drunk instead of beer in gastropubs, but it doesn't say that wine is thought to show one's class and taste. This is an assumption not directly stated in the article, so option B is probably not correct. Option C: British people do not go to pubs as often as before. The article says that this habit (of going to pubs to drink beer and socialize) is slowly changing among some British people. However, it doesn't explicitly say that British people are going to pubs less often overall, just that the nature of what's happening in pubs is changing (more emphasis on food, and some people are closing traditional pubs for more profitable gastropubs). So option C is not directly supported by the article. Option D: Local pubs are being seriously affected by gastropubs.
